NKJV.1: My son, if you become surety for your friend, If you have shaken hands in pledge for a stranger,
NKJV.2: You are snared by the words of your mouth; You are taken by the words of your mouth.
NKJV.3: So do this, my son, and deliver yourself; For you have come into the hand of your friend: Go and humble yourself; Plead with your friend.
NKJV.4: Give no sleep to your eyes, Nor slumber to your eyelids.
NKJV.5: Deliver yourself like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter, And like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
NKJV.6: Go to the ant, you sluggard! Consider her ways and be wise,
NKJV.7: Which, having no captain, Overseer or ruler,
NKJV.8: Provides her supplies in the summer, And gathers her food in the harvest.
NKJV.9: How long will you slumber, O sluggard? When will you rise from your sleep?
NKJV.10: A little sleep, a little slumber, A little folding of the hands to sleep—
NKJV.11: So shall your poverty come on you like a prowler, And your need like an armed man.
NKJV.12: A worthless person, a wicked man, Walks with a perverse mouth;
NKJV.13: He winks with his eyes, He shuffles his feet, He points with his fingers;
NKJV.14: Perversity is in his heart, He devises evil continually, He sows discord.
NKJV.15: Therefore his calamity shall come suddenly; Suddenly he shall be broken without remedy.
NKJV.16: These six things the LORD hates, Yes, seven are an abomination to Him:
NKJV.17: A proud look,  A lying tongue,  Hands that shed innocent blood,
NKJV.18: A heart that devises wicked plans,  Feet that are swift in running to evil,
NKJV.19: A false witness who speaks lies, And one who sows discord among brethren.
NKJV.20: My son, keep your father’s command, And do not forsake the law of your mother.
NKJV.21: Bind them continually upon your heart; Tie them around your neck.
NKJV.22: When you roam, they will lead you; When you sleep, they will keep you; And when you awake, they will speak with you.
NKJV.23: For the commandment is a lamp, And the law a light; Reproofs of instruction are the way of life,
NKJV.24: To keep you from the evil woman, From the flattering tongue of a seductress.
NKJV.25: Do not lust after her beauty in your heart, Nor let her allure you with her eyelids.
NKJV.26: For by means of a harlot A man is reduced to a crust of bread; And an adulteress will prey upon his precious life.
NKJV.27: Can a man take fire to his bosom, And his clothes not be burned?
NKJV.28: Can one walk on hot coals, And his feet not be seared?
NKJV.29: So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife; Whoever touches her shall not be innocent.
NKJV.30: People do not despise a thief If he steals to satisfy himself when he is starving.
NKJV.31: Yet when he is found, he must restore sevenfold; He may have to give up all the substance of his house.
NKJV.32: Whoever commits adultery with a woman lacks understanding; He who does so destroys his own soul.
NKJV.33: Wounds and dishonor he will get, And his reproach will not be wiped away.
NKJV.34: For jealousy is a husband’s fury; Therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
NKJV.35: He will accept no recompense, Nor will he be appeased though you give many gifts.

ESV.1: My son, if you have put up security for your neighbor, have given your pledge for a stranger,
ESV.2: if you are snared in the words of your mouth, caught in the words of your mouth,
ESV.3: then do this, my son, and save yourself, for you have come into the hand of your neighbor: go, hasten,  and plead urgently with your neighbor.
ESV.4: Give your eyes no sleep and your eyelids no slumber;
ESV.5: save yourself like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter,  like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
ESV.6: Go to the ant, O sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise.
ESV.7: Without having any chief, officer, or ruler,
ESV.8: she prepares her bread in summer and gathers her food in harvest.
ESV.9: How long will you lie there, O sluggard? When will you arise from your sleep?
ESV.10: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to rest,
ESV.11: and poverty will come upon you like a robber, and want like an armed man.
ESV.12: A worthless person, a wicked man, goes about with crooked speech,
ESV.13: winks with his eyes, signals  with his feet, points with his finger,
ESV.14: with perverted heart devises evil, continually sowing discord;
ESV.15: therefore calamity will come upon him suddenly; in a moment he will be broken beyond healing.
ESV.16: There are six things that the LORD hates, seven that are an abomination to him:
ESV.17: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,
ESV.18: a heart that devises wicked plans, feet that make haste to run to evil,
ESV.19: a false witness who breathes out lies, and one who sows discord among brothers.
ESV.20: My son, keep your father's commandment, and forsake not your mother's teaching.
ESV.21: Bind them on your heart always; tie them around your neck.
ESV.22: When you walk, they  will lead you; when you lie down, they will watch over you; and when you awake, they will talk with you.
ESV.23: For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching a light, and the reproofs of discipline are the way of life,
ESV.24: to preserve you from the evil woman,  from the smooth tongue of the adulteress.
ESV.25: Do not desire her beauty in your heart, and do not let her capture you with her eyelashes;
ESV.26: for the price of a prostitute is only a loaf of bread,  but a married woman  hunts down a precious life.
ESV.27: Can a man carry fire next to his chest and his clothes not be burned?
ESV.28: Or can one walk on hot coals and his feet not be scorched?
ESV.29: So is he who goes in to his neighbor's wife; none who touches her will go unpunished.
ESV.30: People do not despise a thief if he steals to satisfy his appetite when he is hungry,
ESV.31: but if he is caught, he will pay sevenfold; he will give all the goods of his house.
ESV.32: He who commits adultery lacks sense; he who does it destroys himself.
ESV.33: He will get wounds and dishonor, and his disgrace will not be wiped away.
ESV.34: For jealousy makes a man furious, and he will not spare when he takes revenge.
ESV.35: He will accept no compensation; he will refuse though you multiply gifts.

NASB.1: My son, if you have become surety for your neighbor, Have given a pledge for a stranger,
NASB.2: [If] you have been snared with the words of your mouth, Have been caught with the words of your mouth,
NASB.3: Do this then, my son, and deliver yourself; Since you have come into the hand of your neighbor, Go, humble yourself, and importune your neighbor.
NASB.4: Give no sleep to your eyes, Nor slumber to your eyelids;
NASB.5: Deliver yourself like a gazelle from [the hunter's] hand And like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
NASB.6: Go to the ant, O sluggard, Observe her ways and be wise,
NASB.7: Which, having no chief, Officer or ruler,
NASB.8: Prepares her food in the summer [And] gathers her provision in the harvest.
NASB.9: How long will you lie down, O sluggard? When will you arise from your sleep?
NASB.10: 'A little sleep, a little slumber, A little folding of the hands to rest '--
NASB.11: Your poverty will come in like a vagabond And your need like an armed man.
NASB.12: A worthless person, a wicked man, Is the one who walks with a perverse mouth,
NASB.13: Who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet, Who points with his fingers;
NASB.14: Who [with] perversity in his heart continually devises evil, Who spreads strife.
NASB.15: Therefore his calamity will come suddenly; Instantly he will be broken and there will be no healing.
NASB.16: There are six things which the LORD hates, Yes, seven which are an abomination to Him:
NASB.17: Haughty eyes, a lying tongue, And hands that shed innocent blood,
NASB.18: A heart that devises wicked plans, Feet that run rapidly to evil,
NASB.19: A false witness [who] utters lies, And one who spreads strife among brothers.
NASB.20: My son, observe the commandment of your father And do not forsake the teaching of your mother;
NASB.21: Bind them continually on your heart; Tie them around your neck.
NASB.22: When you walk about, they will guide you; When you sleep, they will watch over you; And when you awake, they will talk to you.
NASB.23: For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching is light; And reproofs for discipline are the way of life
NASB.24: To keep you from the evil woman, From the smooth tongue of the adulteress.
NASB.25: Do not desire her beauty in your heart, Nor let her capture you with her eyelids.
NASB.26: For on account of a harlot [one is reduced] to a loaf of bread, And an adulteress hunts for the precious life.
NASB.27: Can a man take fire in his bosom And his clothes not be burned?
NASB.28: Or can a man walk on hot coals And his feet not be scorched?
NASB.29: So is the one who goes in to his neighbor's wife; Whoever touches her will not go unpunished.
NASB.30: Men do not despise a thief if he steals To satisfy himself when he is hungry;
NASB.31: But when he is found, he must repay sevenfold; He must give all the substance of his house.
NASB.32: The one who commits adultery with a woman is lacking sense; He who would destroy himself does it.
NASB.33: Wounds and disgrace he will find, And his reproach will not be blotted out.
NASB.34: For jealousy enrages a man, And he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
NASB.35: He will not accept any ransom, Nor will he be satisfied though you give many gifts.

NLT.1:
My child, if you have put up security for a friend’s debt
or agreed to guarantee the debt of a stranger —
NLT.2:
if you have trapped yourself by your agreement
and are caught by what you said —
NLT.3:
follow my advice and save yourself,
for you have placed yourself at your friend’s mercy.
Now swallow your pride;
go and beg to have your name erased.
NLT.4:
Don’t put it off; do it now!
Don’t rest until you do.
NLT.5:
Save yourself like a gazelle escaping from a hunter,
like a bird fleeing from a net.
NLT.6:
Take a lesson from the ants, you lazybones.
Learn from their ways and become wise!
NLT.7:
Though they have no prince
or governor or ruler to make them work,
NLT.8:
they labor hard all summer,
gathering food for the winter.
NLT.9:
But you, lazybones, how long will you sleep?
When will you wake up?
NLT.10:
A little extra sleep, a little more slumber,
a little folding of the hands to rest —
NLT.11:
then poverty will pounce on you like a bandit;
scarcity will attack you like an armed robber.
NLT.12:
What are worthless and wicked people like?
They are constant liars,
NLT.13:
signaling their deceit with a wink of the eye,
a nudge of the foot, or the wiggle of fingers.
NLT.14:
Their perverted hearts plot evil,
and they constantly stir up trouble.
NLT.15:
But they will be destroyed suddenly,
broken in an instant beyond all hope of healing.
NLT.16:
There are six things the LORD hates —
no, seven things he detests:
NLT.17:
haughty eyes,
a lying tongue,
hands that kill the innocent,
NLT.18:
a heart that plots evil,
feet that race to do wrong,
NLT.19:
a false witness who pours out lies,
a person who sows discord in a family.
NLT.20:
My son, obey your father’s commands,
and don’t neglect your mother’s instruction.
NLT.21:
Keep their words always in your heart.
Tie them around your neck.
NLT.22:
When you walk, their counsel will lead you.
When you sleep, they will protect you.
When you wake up, they will advise you.
NLT.23:
For their command is a lamp
and their instruction a light;
their corrective discipline
is the way to life.
NLT.24:
It will keep you from the immoral woman,
from the smooth tongue of a promiscuous woman.
NLT.25:
Don’t lust for her beauty.
Don’t let her coy glances seduce you.
NLT.26:
For a prostitute will bring you to poverty,
but sleeping with another man’s wife will cost you your life.
NLT.27:
Can a man scoop a flame into his lap
and not have his clothes catch on fire?
NLT.28:
Can he walk on hot coals
and not blister his feet?
NLT.29:
So it is with the man who sleeps with another man’s wife.
He who embraces her will not go unpunished.
NLT.30:
Excuses might be found for a thief
who steals because he is starving.
NLT.31:
But if he is caught, he must pay back seven times what he stole,
even if he has to sell everything in his house.
NLT.32:
But the man who commits adultery is an utter fool,
for he destroys himself.
NLT.33:
He will be wounded and disgraced.
His shame will never be erased.
NLT.34:
For the woman’s jealous husband will be furious,
and he will show no mercy when he takes revenge.
NLT.35:
He will accept no compensation,
nor be satisfied with a payoff of any size.

WEB.1: My son, if you have become collateral for your neighbor, if you have struck your hands in pledge for a stranger;
WEB.2: You are trapped by the words of your mouth. You are ensnared with the words of your mouth.
WEB.3: Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself, since you have come into the hand of your neighbor. Go, humble yourself. Press your plea with your neighbor.
WEB.4: Give no sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids.
WEB.5: Free yourself, like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter, like a bird from the snare of the fowler.
WEB.6: Go to the ant, you sluggard. Consider her ways, and be wise;
WEB.7: which having no chief, overseer, or ruler,
WEB.8: provides her bread in the summer, and gathers her food in the harvest.
WEB.9: How long will you sleep, sluggard? When will you arise out of your sleep?
WEB.10: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:
WEB.11: so your poverty will come as a robber, and your scarcity as an armed man.
WEB.12: A worthless person, a man of iniquity, is he who walks with a perverse mouth;
WEB.13: who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet, who motions with his fingers;
WEB.14: in whose heart is perverseness, who devises evil continually, who always sows discord.
WEB.15: Therefore his calamity will come suddenly. He will be broken suddenly, and that without remedy.
WEB.16: There are six things which Yahweh hates; yes, seven which are an abomination to him:
WEB.17: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood;
WEB.18: a heart that devises wicked schemes, feet that are swift in running to mischief,
WEB.19: a false witness who utters lies, and he who sows discord among brothers.
WEB.20: My son, keep your father’s commandment, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching.
WEB.21: Bind them continually on your heart. Tie them around your neck.
WEB.22: When you walk, it will lead you. When you sleep, it will watch over you. When you awake, it will talk with you.
WEB.23: For the commandment is a lamp, and the law is light. Reproofs of instruction are the way of life,
WEB.24: to keep you from the immoral woman, from the flattery of the wayward wife’s tongue.
WEB.25: Don’t lust after her beauty in your heart, neither let her captivate you with her eyelids.
WEB.26: For a prostitute reduces you to a piece of bread. The adulteress hunts for your precious life.
WEB.27: Can a man scoop fire into his lap, and his clothes not be burned?
WEB.28: Or can one walk on hot coals, and his feet not be scorched?
WEB.29: So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife. Whoever touches her will not be unpunished.
WEB.30: Men don’t despise a thief, if he steals to satisfy himself when he is hungry:
WEB.31: but if he is found, he shall restore seven times. He shall give all the wealth of his house.
WEB.32: He who commits adultery with a woman is void of understanding. He who does it destroys his own soul.
WEB.33: He will get wounds and dishonor. His reproach will not be wiped away.
WEB.34: For jealousy arouses the fury of the husband. He won’t spare in the day of vengeance.
WEB.35: He won’t regard any ransom, neither will he rest content, though you give many gifts.

CSB17.15:
Therefore calamity will strike him suddenly;
he will be shattered instantly, beyond recovery. ⓚ
CSB17.16:
The LORD hates six things;
in fact, seven are detestable to him:
CSB17.17:
arrogant eyes, ⓛ a lying tongue, ⓜ
hands that shed innocent blood, ⓝ
CSB17.18:
a heart that plots wicked schemes, ⓐ
feet eager to run to evil, ⓑ
CSB17.19:
a lying witness who gives false testimony, ⓒ
and one who stirs up trouble among brothers. ⓓ
CSB17.20:
My son, keep your father’s command,
and don’t reject your mother’s teaching. ⓔ
CSB17.21:
Always bind them to your heart;
tie them around your neck. ⓕ
CSB17.22:
When you walk here and there, they will guide you;
when you lie down, they will watch over you;
when you wake up, they will talk to you.
CSB17.23:
For a command is a lamp, teaching is a light, ⓖ
and corrective discipline is the way to life. ⓗ
CSB17.24:
They will protect you from an evil woman, [5]
from the flattering [6] tongue of a wayward woman. ⓘ
CSB17.25:
Don’t lust in your heart for her beauty ⓙ
or let her captivate you with her eyelashes. ⓚ
CSB17.26:
For a prostitute’s fee is only a loaf of bread, [7]
but the wife of another man [8] goes after a precious life. ⓛ
CSB17.27:
Can a man embrace fire
and his clothes not be burned?
CSB17.28:
Can a man walk on burning coals
without scorching his feet?
CSB17.29:
So it is with the one who sleeps with
another man’s wife;
no one who touches her will go unpunished. ⓜ
CSB17.30:
People don’t despise the thief if he steals
to satisfy himself when he is hungry. ⓝ
CSB17.31:
Still, if caught, he must pay seven times as much; ⓐ
he must give up all the wealth in his house.
CSB17.32:
The one who commits adultery [9] lacks sense;
whoever does so destroys himself. ⓑ
CSB17.33:
He will get a beating [10] and dishonor,
and his disgrace will never be removed.
CSB17.34:
For jealousy enrages a husband, ⓒ
and he will show no mercy when he takes revenge.
CSB17.35:
He will not be appeased by anything
or be persuaded by lavish bribes.

AMP.1:
My son, if you have become surety (guaranteed a debt or obligation) for your neighbor,
If you have given your pledge for [the debt of] a stranger or another [outside your family],
AMP.2:
If you have been snared with the words of your lips,
If you have been trapped by the speech of your mouth,
AMP.3:
Do this now, my son, and release yourself [from the obligation];
Since you have come into the hand of your neighbor,
Go humble yourself, and plead with your neighbor [to pay his debt and release you].
AMP.4:
Give no [unnecessary] sleep to your eyes,
Nor slumber to your eyelids;
AMP.5:
Tear yourself away like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter
And like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
AMP.6:
Go to the ant, O lazy one;
Observe her ways and be wise,
AMP.7:
Which, having no chief,
Overseer or ruler,
AMP.8:
She prepares her food in the summer
And brings in her provisions [of food for the winter] in the harvest.
AMP.9:
How long will you lie down, O lazy one?
When will you arise from your sleep [and learn self-discipline]?
AMP.10:
"Yet a little sleep, a little slumber,
A little folding of the hands to lie down and rest"—
AMP.11:
So your poverty will come like an approaching prowler who walks [slowly, but surely]
And your need [will come] like an armed man [making you helpless].
AMP.12:
A worthless person, a wicked man,
Is one who walks with a perverse (corrupt, vulgar) mouth.
AMP.13:
Who winks with his eyes [in mockery], who shuffles his feet [to signal],
Who points with his fingers [to give subversive instruction];
AMP.14:
Who perversely in his heart plots trouble and evil continually;
Who spreads discord and strife.
AMP.15:
Therefore [the crushing weight of] his disaster will come suddenly upon him;
Instantly he will be broken, and there will be no healing or remedy [because he has no heart for God].
AMP.16:
These six things the LORD hates;
Indeed, seven are repulsive to Him:
AMP.17:
A proud look [the attitude that makes one overestimate oneself and discount others], a lying tongue,
And hands that shed innocent blood,
AMP.18:
A heart that creates wicked plans,
Feet that run swiftly to evil,
AMP.19:
A false witness who breathes out lies [even half-truths],
And one who spreads discord (rumors) among brothers.
AMP.20:
My son, be guided by your father's [God-given] commandment (instruction)
And do not reject the teaching of your mother;
AMP.21:
Bind them continually upon your heart (in your thoughts),
And tie them around your neck.
AMP.22:
When you walk about, they (the godly teachings of your parents) will guide you;
When you sleep, they will keep watch over you;
And when you awake, they will talk to you.
AMP.23:
For the commandment is a lamp, and the teaching [of the law] is light,
And reproofs (rebukes) for discipline are the way of life,
AMP.24:
To keep you from the evil woman,
From [the flattery of] the smooth tongue of an immoral woman.
AMP.25:
Do not desire (lust after) her beauty in your heart,
Nor let her capture you with her eyelashes.
AMP.26:
For on account of a prostitute one is reduced to a piece of bread [to be eaten up],
And the immoral woman hunts [with a hook] the precious life [of a man].
AMP.27:
Can a man take fire to his chest
And his clothes not be burned?
AMP.28:
Or can a man walk on hot coals
And his feet not be scorched?
AMP.29:
So is the one who goes in to his neighbor's wife;
Whoever touches her will not be found innocent or go unpunished.
AMP.30:
People do not despise a thief if he steals
To satisfy himself when he is hungry;
AMP.31:
But when he is found, he must repay seven times [what he stole];
He must give all the property of his house [if necessary to meet his fine].
AMP.32:
But whoever commits adultery with a woman lacks common sense and sound judgment and an understanding [of moral principles];
He who would destroy his soul does it.
AMP.33:
Wounds and disgrace he will find,
And his reproach (blame) will not be blotted out.
AMP.34:
For jealousy enrages the [wronged] husband;
He will not spare [the guilty one] on the day of vengeance.
AMP.35:
He will not accept any ransom [offered to buy him off from demanding full punishment];
Nor will he be satisfied though you offer him many gifts (bribes).

MSG.1:
Dear friend, if you've gone into hock with your neighbor
or locked yourself into a deal with a stranger,
MSG.2:
If you've impulsively promised the shirt off your back
and now find yourself shivering out in the cold,
MSG.3:
Friend, don't waste a minute, get yourself out of that mess.
You're in that man's clutches!
Go, put on a long face; act desperate.
MSG.4:
Don't procrastinate—
there's no time to lose.
MSG.5:
Run like a deer from the hunter,
fly like a bird from the trapper!
MSG.6:
You lazy fool, look at an ant.
Watch it closely; let it teach you a thing or two.
MSG.7: Nobody has to tell it what to do.
MSG.8:
All summer it stores up food;
at harvest it stockpiles provisions.
MSG.9:
So how long are you going to laze around doing nothing?
How long before you get out of bed?
MSG.10:
A nap here, a nap there, a day off here, a day off there,
sit back, take it easy—do you know what comes next?
MSG.11:
Just this: You can look forward to a dirt-poor life,
poverty your permanent houseguest!
MSG.12:
Riffraff and rascals
talk out of both sides of their mouths.
MSG.13:
They wink at each other, they shuffle their feet,
they cross their fingers behind their backs.
MSG.14:
Their perverse minds are always cooking up something nasty,
always stirring up trouble.
MSG.15:
Catastrophe is just around the corner for them,
a total smash-up, their lives ruined beyond repair.
MSG.16:
Here are six things God hates,
and one more that he loathes with a passion:
MSG.17:
eyes that are arrogant,
a tongue that lies,
hands that murder the innocent,
MSG.18:
a heart that hatches evil plots,
feet that race down a wicked track,
MSG.19:
a mouth that lies under oath,
a troublemaker in the family.
MSG.20:
Good friend, follow your father's good advice;
don't wander off from your mother's teachings.
MSG.21:
Wrap yourself in them from head to foot;
wear them like a scarf around your neck.
MSG.22:
Wherever you walk, they'll guide you;
whenever you rest, they'll guard you;
when you wake up, they'll tell you what's next.
MSG.23:
For sound advice is a beacon,
good teaching is a light,
moral discipline is a life path.
MSG.24:
They'll protect you from wanton women,
from the seductive talk of some temptress.
MSG.25:
Don't lustfully fantasize on her beauty,
nor be taken in by her bedroom eyes.
MSG.26:
You can buy an hour with a whore for a loaf of bread,
but a wanton woman may well eat you alive.
MSG.27:
Can you build a fire in your lap
and not burn your pants?
MSG.28:
Can you walk barefoot on hot coals
and not get blisters?
MSG.29:
It's the same when you have sex with your neighbor's wife:
Touch her and you'll pay for it. No excuses.
MSG.30:
Hunger is no excuse
for a thief to steal;
MSG.31:
When he's caught he has to pay it back,
even if he has to put his whole house in hock.
MSG.32:
Adultery is a brainless act,
soul-destroying, self-destructive;
MSG.33:
Expect a bloody nose, a black eye,
and a reputation ruined for good.
MSG.34:
For jealousy detonates rage in a cheated husband;
wild for revenge, he won't make allowances.
MSG.35:
Nothing you say or pay will make it all right;
neither bribes nor reason will satisfy him.

NIV.1:
Warnings Against Folly
My son, if you have put up security for your neighbor,
if you have struck hands in pledge for another,
NIV.2:
if you have been trapped by what you said,
ensnared by the words of your mouth,
NIV.3:
then do this, my son, to free yourself,
since you have fallen into your neighbor’s hands:
Go and humble yourself;
press your plea with your neighbor!
NIV.4:
Allow no sleep to your eyes,
no slumber to your eyelids.
NIV.5:
Free yourself, like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter,
like a bird from the snare of the fowler.
NIV.6:
Go to the ant, you sluggard;
consider its ways and be wise!
NIV.7:
It has no commander,
no overseer or ruler,
NIV.8:
yet it stores its provisions in summer
and gathers its food at harvest.
NIV.9:
How long will you lie there, you sluggard?
When will you get up from your sleep?
NIV.10:
A little sleep, a little slumber,
a little folding of the hands to rest—
NIV.11:
and poverty will come on you like a bandit
and scarcity like an armed man.
NIV.12:
A scoundrel and villain,
who goes about with a corrupt mouth,
NIV.13:
who winks with his eye,
signals with his feet
and motions with his fingers,
NIV.14:
who plots evil with deceit in his heart—
he always stirs up dissension.
NIV.15:
Therefore disaster will overtake him in an instant;
he will suddenly be destroyed—without remedy.
NIV.16:
There are six things the Lord hates,
seven that are detestable to him:
NIV.17:
haughty eyes,
a lying tongue,
hands that shed innocent blood,
NIV.18:
a heart that devises wicked schemes,
feet that are quick to rush into evil,
NIV.19:
a false witness who pours out lies
and a man who stirs up dissension among brothers.
NIV.20:
Warning Against Adultery
My son, keep your father’s commands
and do not forsake your mother’s teaching.
NIV.21:
Bind them upon your heart forever;
fasten them around your neck.
NIV.22:
When you walk, they will guide you;
when you sleep, they will watch over you;
when you awake, they will speak to you.
NIV.23:
For these commands are a lamp,
this teaching is a light,
and the corrections of discipline
are the way to life,
NIV.24:
keeping you from the immoral woman,
from the smooth tongue of the wayward wife.
NIV.25:
Do not lust in your heart after her beauty
or let her captivate you with her eyes,
NIV.26:
for the prostitute reduces you to a loaf of bread,
and the adulteress preys upon your very life.
NIV.27:
Can a man scoop fire into his lap
without his clothes being burned?
NIV.28:
Can a man walk on hot coals
without his feet being scorched?
NIV.29:
So is he who sleeps with another man’s wife;
no one who touches her will go unpunished.
NIV.30:
Men do not despise a thief if he steals
to satisfy his hunger when he is starving.
NIV.31:
Yet if he is caught, he must pay sevenfold,
though it costs him all the wealth of his house.
NIV.32:
But a man who commits adultery lacks judgment;
whoever does so destroys himself.
NIV.33:
Blows and disgrace are his lot,
and his shame will never be wiped away;
NIV.34:
for jealousy arouses a husband’s fury,
and he will show no mercy when he takes revenge.
NIV.35:
He will not accept any compensation;
he will refuse the bribe, however great it is.

NRSVCE.1:
My child, if you have given your pledge to your neighbor,
if you have bound yourself to another,
NRSVCE.2:
you are snared by the utterance of your lips,
caught by the words of your mouth.
NRSVCE.3:
So do this, my child, and save yourself,
for you have come into your neighbor’s power:
go, hurry, and plead with your neighbor.
NRSVCE.4:
Give your eyes no sleep
and your eyelids no slumber;
NRSVCE.5:
save yourself like a gazelle from the hunter,
like a bird from the hand of the fowler.
NRSVCE.6:
Go to the ant, you lazybones;
consider its ways, and be wise.
NRSVCE.7:
Without having any chief
or officer or ruler,
NRSVCE.8:
it prepares its food in summer,
and gathers its sustenance in harvest.
NRSVCE.9:
How long will you lie there, O lazybones?
When will you rise from your sleep?
NRSVCE.10:
A little sleep, a little slumber,
a little folding of the hands to rest,
NRSVCE.11:
and poverty will come upon you like a robber,
and want, like an armed warrior.
NRSVCE.12:
A scoundrel and a villain
goes around with crooked speech,
NRSVCE.13:
winking the eyes, shuffling the feet,
pointing the fingers,
NRSVCE.14:
with perverted mind devising evil,
continually sowing discord;
NRSVCE.15:
on such a one calamity will descend suddenly;
in a moment, damage beyond repair.
NRSVCE.16:
There are six things that the LORD hates,
seven that are an abomination to him:
NRSVCE.17:
haughty eyes, a lying tongue,
and hands that shed innocent blood,
NRSVCE.18:
a heart that devises wicked plans,
feet that hurry to run to evil,
NRSVCE.19:
a lying witness who testifies falsely,
and one who sows discord in a family.
NRSVCE.20:
My child, keep your father’s commandment,
and do not forsake your mother’s teaching.
NRSVCE.21:
Bind them upon your heart always;
tie them around your neck.
NRSVCE.22:
When you walk, they will lead you;
when you lie down, they will watch over you;
and when you awake, they will talk with you.
NRSVCE.23:
For the commandment is a lamp and the teaching a light,
and the reproofs of discipline are the way of life,
NRSVCE.24:
to preserve you from the wife of another,
from the smooth tongue of the adulteress.
NRSVCE.25:
Do not desire her beauty in your heart,
and do not let her capture you with her eyelashes;
NRSVCE.26:
for a prostitute’s fee is only a loaf of bread,
but the wife of another stalks a man’s very life.
NRSVCE.27:
Can fire be carried in the bosom
without burning one’s clothes?
NRSVCE.28:
Or can one walk on hot coals
without scorching the feet?
NRSVCE.29:
So is he who sleeps with his neighbor’s wife;
no one who touches her will go unpunished.
NRSVCE.30:
Thieves are not despised who steal only
to satisfy their appetite when they are hungry.
NRSVCE.31:
Yet if they are caught, they will pay sevenfold;
they will forfeit all the goods of their house.
NRSVCE.32:
But he who commits adultery has no sense;
he who does it destroys himself.
NRSVCE.33:
He will get wounds and dishonor,
and his disgrace will not be wiped away.
NRSVCE.34:
For jealousy arouses a husband’s fury,
and he shows no restraint when he takes revenge.
NRSVCE.35:
He will accept no compensation,
and refuses a bribe no matter how great.

NET.1: My child, if you have made a pledge for your neighbor, and have become a guarantor for a stranger,
NET.2: if you have been ensnared by the words you have uttered, and have been caught by the words you have spoken,
NET.3: then, my child, do this in order to deliver yourself, because you have fallen into your neighbor’s power: go, humble yourself, and appeal firmly to your neighbor.
NET.4: Permit no sleep to your eyes or slumber to your eyelids.
NET.5: Deliver yourself like a gazelle from a snare, and like a bird from the trap of the fowler.
NET.6: Go to the ant, you sluggard; observe its ways and be wise!
NET.7: It has no commander, overseer, or ruler,
NET.8: yet it prepares its food in the summer; it gathers at the harvest what it will eat.
NET.9: How long, you sluggard, will you lie there? When will you rise from your sleep?
NET.10: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to relax,
NET.11: and your poverty will come like a robber, and your need like an armed man.
NET.12: A worthless and wicked person walks around saying perverse things;
NET.13: he winks with his eyes, signals with his feet, and points with his fingers;
NET.14: he plots evil with perverse thoughts in his heart, he spreads contention at all times.
NET.15: Therefore, his disaster will come suddenly; in an instant he will be broken, and there will be no remedy.
NET.16: There are six things that the LORD hates, even seven things that are an abomination to him:
NET.17: haughty eyes, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,
NET.18: a heart that devises wicked plans, feet that are swift to run to evil,
NET.19: a false witness who pours out lies, and a person who spreads discord among family members.
NET.20: My child, guard the commands of your father and do not forsake the instruction of your mother.
NET.21: Bind them on your heart continually; fasten them around your neck.
NET.22: When you walk about, they will guide you; when you lie down, they will watch over you; when you wake up, they will talk to you.
NET.23: For the commandments are like a lamp, instruction is like a light, and rebukes of discipline are like the road leading to life,
NET.24: by keeping you from the evil woman, from the smooth tongue of the loose woman.
NET.25: Do not lust in your heart for her beauty, and do not let her captivate you with her alluring eyes;
NET.26: for on account of a prostitute one is brought down to a loaf of bread, but the wife of another man preys on your precious life.
NET.27: Can a man hold fire against his chest without burning his clothes?
NET.28: Can a man walk on hot coals without scorching his feet?
NET.29: So it is with the one who has sex with his neighbor’s wife; no one who touches her will escape punishment.
NET.30: People do not despise a thief when he steals to fulfill his need when he is hungry.
NET.31: Yet if he is caught he must repay seven times over, he might even have to give all the wealth of his house.
NET.32: A man who commits adultery with a woman lacks wisdom, whoever does it destroys his own life.
NET.33: He will be beaten and despised, and his reproach will not be wiped away;
NET.34: for jealousy kindles a husband’s rage, and he will not show mercy when he takes revenge.
NET.35: He will not consider any compensation; he will not be willing, even if you multiply the compensation.
